Package neqsim.process.safety.dispersion

Class GaussianPlume

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.safety.dispersion.GaussianPlume
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class GaussianPlume extends Object implements Serializable
Pasquill–Gifford Gaussian plume dispersion model for neutral / passive gas releases.

Implements the steady-state Gaussian plume centerline and ground-level concentration formulae (Briggs urban / rural sigma coefficients) for the six Pasquill atmospheric stability classes (A–F).

References:

  • EPA-454/R-99-005 — Industrial Source Complex (ISC) Dispersion Models, Vol II
  • Briggs G.A. (1973) — Diffusion estimation for small emissions
  • CCPS — Guidelines for Consequence Analysis of Chemical Releases
Version:
1.0
Author:
ESOL

  • Constructor Details

    • GaussianPlume

      public GaussianPlume(double emissionRateKgPerS, double effectiveStackHeightM, double windSpeedAtRefM10m, GaussianPlume.Stability stability, GaussianPlume.Terrain terrain)
      Construct a Gaussian plume model.
      Parameters:
      emissionRateKgPerS - continuous source strength in kg/s
      effectiveStackHeightM - effective release height (stack + buoyancy rise) in m
      windSpeedAtRefM10m - wind speed at 10 m reference in m/s
      stability - Pasquill atmospheric stability class
      terrain - rural or urban terrain

  • Method Details

    • sigmaY

      public double sigmaY(double x)
      Horizontal dispersion coefficient (Briggs).
      Parameters:
      x - downwind distance in m (must be positive)
      Returns:
      σy in m

    • sigmaZ

      public double sigmaZ(double x)
      Vertical dispersion coefficient (Briggs).
      Parameters:
      x - downwind distance in m
      Returns:
      σz in m

    • briggsY

      private double[] briggsY()

    • briggsZ

      private double[] briggsZ()

    • concentration

      public double concentration(double x, double y, double z)
      Compute concentration at point (x downwind, y crosswind, z height).
      Parameters:
      x - downwind distance in m (> 0)
      y - crosswind distance in m
      z - receptor height in m
      Returns:
      concentration in kg/m³

    • centerlineGroundConcentration

      public double centerlineGroundConcentration(double x)
      Compute ground-level centerline concentration directly downwind.
      Parameters:
      x - downwind distance in m
      Returns:
      concentration in kg/m³

    • distanceToConcentration

      public double distanceToConcentration(double thresholdKgPerM3)
      Estimate the maximum downwind distance at which the centerline ground concentration drops to the supplied threshold (e.g. LFL, IDLH, ERPG-2).
      Parameters:
      thresholdKgPerM3 - concentration threshold in kg/m³
      Returns:
      downwind distance in m, or Double.NaN if never reached
